name: Arman Shehabi
description: Lead researcher for U.S. data center energy usage reports — the primary benchmark source for AI power demand estimates.

name: Jesse Jenkins
description: Runs the REPEAT project — real-time modeling of how U.S. energy policy converts to physical infrastructure and employment outcomes.

name: Ari Peskoe
topics: utility regulation|ratepayer costs|FERC|data center cost allocation
description: Author of Extracting Profits from the Public — documents how utility rate structures shift data center costs onto residential ratepayers. Core source for DD-004.

name: David Autor
description: Leading labor economist on automation and the future of work; his NBER work on AI and middle-class jobs is a primary reference for DD-003.

name: Brad Setser
topics: trade policy|capital flows|industrial policy|balance of payments
description: CFR fellow tracking international capital flows and trade policy with primary source rigor — essential when analysis touches tariffs or semiconductor supply chain geography.

name: David Roberts
description: Long-form newsletter and podcast on energy policy mechanics — goes deeper on regulatory and grid topics than almost any general-audience outlet.

name: Robinson Meyer
description: Founding editor of Heatmap News; co-hosts Shift Key podcast with Jesse Jenkins — bridges accessible journalism and rigorous energy policy analysis.

name: Brian Potter
description: Covers why physical things are expensive and slow to build — essential for grounding any analysis of manufacturing capacity or infrastructure timelines.

name: Adam Tooze
description: Historian turned political economist; writes about how capital flows and policy create durable path dependencies — closest analytical frame to this project.

name: Noah Smith
description: Former Bloomberg columnist; covers industrial policy and the physical consequences of technology investment with accessible but rigorous analysis.

name: Matt Stoller
description: Covers corporate concentration and industrial policy — useful for supply chain and market structure questions in the equipment and materials nodes.

name: Lynne Kiesling
description: Energy economist covering electricity market design and regulation; her Paying for Power in the Age of AI piece addresses the cost allocation question at the center of DD-004.

name: Doomberg
description: Anonymous Substack on energy markets and commodity economics; contrarian and rigorous on the gap between policy ambition and physical reality.

name: Kyla Scanlon
description: Economics creator with 1M+ followers across platforms — exemplary model for making complex economic dynamics legible to a general audience.

name: Hannah Ritchie
affiliation: Our World in Data / University of Oxford
description: Head of Research at Our World in Data; Substack applies data rigor to sustainability questions — strong model for evidence-based communication on energy and climate.

name: John Burn-Murdoch
description: Chief Data Reporter at the FT; one of the leading practitioners of insight-first data visualization — his approach of leading with the finding maps directly to SWD principles.

name: Carbon Brief
description: UK outlet specialising in data-driven climate and energy journalism; award-winning visualization of complex policy and scientific data. Free and rigorously sourced.

name: Nathan Yau
description: Statistician and data visualization practitioner; tutorials and commentary on making statistical data understandable — strong on chart design fundamentals.

name: Lisa Charlotte Muth
description: Data visualization designer at Datawrapper; practical evidence-based guides on chart design and color — directly applicable to the chart review process in this project.

name: Cole Nussbaumer Knaflic
description: Author of Storytelling with Data; the SWD methodology (gray+accent color strategy and insight titles) is the explicit standard for all charts in this project.

name: Alberto Cairo
description: Author of The Functional Art and How Charts Lie; foundational theory on how visualization communicates and misleads — essential for rigorous data journalism.

name: The Pudding
description: Digital publication producing visual essays with innovative data storytelling formats — exemplary model for long-form data-driven narrative journalism.

name: Ira Regmi
topics: finance capital|AI infrastructure|political economy|state power|off-balance-sheet financing
description: Political economist writing on the fusion of tech monopolies and financial capital; his series on how finance capital fuels AI infrastructure (Meta Beignet SPV analysis) is a primary source for DD-001's off-balance-sheet financing layer.

name: Daniela Gabor
topics: macrofinance|state derisking|climate finance|Wall Street Consensus|finance capital
description: Professor of macrofinance at UWE Bristol; her Wall Street Consensus framework — how states de-risk private capital to mobilize it for infrastructure investment — is the theoretical backbone for understanding how AI and clean energy infrastructure financing works at the system level.
